document.help: Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under one.
document.title: FlowProvider is missing a runtime
document.why: FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for this React subtree.

missingProvider.message:
[FLOW-REACT-001] FlowProvider is missing a runtime
what happened: useFlowRuntime() was called outside FlowProvider.
why: FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for this React subtree.
help: Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under one.
debug: {"hook":"useFlowRuntime"}
missingProvider.pretty:
FLOW-REACT-001 FlowProvider is missing a runtime

What happened
  useFlowRuntime() was called outside FlowProvider.

Why
  FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for this React subtree.

Help
  Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under one.

Debug
  {"hook":"useFlowRuntime"}

document.help: Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the trigger, or switch concurrency.
document.summary: Flow tried to start 'transactions.save' while another reject-while-running attempt was still active.
document.title: Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running
document.why: The policy kept the active attempt instead of queueing, canceling, or allowing overlap.

rejectedWhileRunningTransaction.message:
[FLOW-TXN-001] Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running
what happened: Flow tried to start 'transactions.save' while another reject-while-running attempt was still active.
why: The policy kept the active attempt instead of queueing, canceling, or allowing overlap.
help: Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the trigger, or switch concurrency.
debug: {"activeAttemptCount":1,"concurrency":"reject-while-running","parentState":"ready","transactionId":"transactions.save"}
rejectedWhileRunningTransaction.pretty:
FLOW-TXN-001 Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running

What happened
  Flow tried to start 'transactions.save' while another reject-while-running attempt was still active.

Why
  The policy kept the active attempt instead of queueing, canceling, or allowing overlap.

Help
  Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the trigger, or switch concurrency.

Debug
  {"activeAttemptCount":1,"concurrency":"reject-while-running","parentState":"ready","transactionId":"transactions.save"}

document.help: Return values instead of throwing. Fail commit work inside the returned Effect or die.
document.summary: Flow called 'params' for transaction 'transactions.save', and it threw before the work settled.
document.title: Transaction callback 'params' threw for 'transactions.save'
document.why: Transaction callbacks run synchronously while Flow resolves params, previews, invalidations, and commit Effects.

document.help: Return an event instead of throwing. Put richer handling behind that event.

document.title: Transaction outcome callback 'routes.success' threw for 'transactions.save'
document.why: Outcome routes run synchronously while Flow maps completion lanes into machine events.

document.help: Check pressure?.strategy before calling this helper.
document.summary: Flow asked 'streams.tokens' for a coalesced pressure key with strategy 'queue'.
document.title: Coalesced pressure requires 'coalesce-latest' for 'streams.tokens'

coalescedStreamPressure.message:
[FLOW-STREAM-002] Coalesced pressure requires 'coalesce-latest' for 'streams.tokens'
what happened: Flow asked 'streams.tokens' for a coalesced pressure key with strategy 'queue'.
why: Only 'coalesce-latest' pressure works here.
help: Check pressure?.strategy before calling this helper.
debug: {"strategy":"queue","streamId":"streams.tokens"}
coalescedStreamPressure.pretty:

What happened

Why

Help

Debug

document.help: Dehydrate again with a matching Flow State runtime version before hydrating this payload.
document.summary: Flow tried to hydrate a runtime boot payload whose version tag does not match the supported runtime boot format.
document.title: Unsupported runtime boot payload version 'flow-state/runtime-boot.v999'
document.why: Runtime boot payloads are versioned so incompatible resource and actor snapshot formats fail closed instead of restoring partial state.

invalidRuntimeBootPayloadVersion.message:
[FLOW-RUNTIME-001] Unsupported runtime boot payload version 'flow-state/runtime-boot.v999'
what happened: Flow tried to hydrate a runtime boot payload whose version tag does not match the supported runtime boot format.
why: Runtime boot payloads are versioned so incompatible resource and actor snapshot formats fail closed instead of restoring partial state.
help: Dehydrate again with a matching Flow State runtime version before hydrating this payload.
debug: {"expectedVersion":"flow-state/runtime-boot.v1","receivedVersion":"flow-state/runtime-boot.v999"}
invalidRuntimeBootPayloadVersion.pretty:
FLOW-RUNTIME-001 Unsupported runtime boot payload version 'flow-state/runtime-boot.v999'

What happened
  Flow tried to hydrate a runtime boot payload whose version tag does not match the supported runtime boot format.

Why
  Runtime boot payloads are versioned so incompatible resource and actor snapshot formats fail closed instead of restoring partial state.

Help
  Dehydrate again with a matching Flow State runtime version before hydrating this payload.

Debug
  {"expectedVersion":"flow-state/runtime-boot.v1","receivedVersion":"flow-state/runtime-boot.v999"}

document.help: Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.
document.summary: The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.
document.title: Missing owned child actor for child.editor
document.why: The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.

missingOwnedChildBug.message:
[bug[flow-orch/missing-owned-child-actor]] Missing owned child actor for child.editor
what happened: The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.
why: The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.
help: Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.
debug: {"childId":"child.editor"}
missingOwnedChildBug.pretty:
bug[flow-orch/missing-owned-child-actor] Missing owned child actor for child.editor

What happened
  The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.

Why
  The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.

Help
  Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.

Debug
  {"childId":"child.editor"}

document.help: Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children.
document.summary: flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.
document.title: flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0
document.why: The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of hiding live fibers.

settleMaxFibers.message:
[FLOW-TEST-001] flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0
what happened: flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.
why: The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of hiding live fibers.
help: Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children.
debug: {"activeFibers":1,"bounds":{"maxFibers":0,"maxTicks":4},"children":[],"mailboxes":[],"nextAfterMillis":2000,"ready":0,"streams":[],"timers":[{"dueAt":2000,"id":"settle.fibers.dismiss","parentState":"waiting"}],"transactions":[]}
settleMaxFibers.pretty:
FLOW-TEST-001 flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0

What happened
  flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.

Why
  The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of hiding live fibers.

Help
  Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children.

Debug
  {"activeFibers":1,"bounds":{"maxFibers":0,"maxTicks":4},"children":[],"mailboxes":[],"nextAfterMillis":2000,"ready":0,"streams":[],"timers":[{"dueAt":2000,"id":"settle.fibers.dismiss","parentState":"waiting"}],"transactions":[]}
